title: coprosma spathulata subsp. spathulata
intro: Coprosma spathulata subsp. spathulata, also known as the 'Kakabeak', is a small, flowering plant native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional Maori medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Coprosma spathulata subsp. spathulata are attributed to its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, making it beneficial for health issues such as arthritis and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant, often in the form of infusions or tinctures, have been used to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
